data.title: QuickLogic announces the amendment and extension of credit facility
data.description:
• QuickLogic (NASDAQ:QUIK) said on Thursday that, effective March 17, 2025, it entered into an eighth amendment to its amended and restated credit facility, dated as of December 21, 2018, to maintain the line of credit and extend the maturity date.
  • The eighth amendment maintains the credit facility of $20M and extends the maturity date from December 31, 2025, to December 31, 2026.

data.title: Stock index futures fall as Fed-driven rally fades
data.description:
Stock index futures reversed course and fell on Thursday, as traders assessed the Federal Reserve's growth and inflation outlooks.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.19%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 5 basis points to 3.94%.

U.S. stocks pulled back from their session highs on Wednesday, but still ended solidly higher after Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell suggested that inflation caused by tariffs would only be "transitory."

"Tariffs will raise inflation and then lower growth, so the pattern is not unexpected, and of course there is considerable uncertainty given erratic government policy," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Federal Reserve officials stuck to their projection of two 25-basis-point interest-rate reductions this year while baking in slower economic growth and higher inflation, according to the central bank's updated Summary of Economic Projections published on Wednesday.

"Our U.S. economists think that while the Fed may be anticipating a slowdown in the hard data, they are on hold until that evidence emerges," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

A clear majority of the FOMC now see risks tilted towards higher inflation but towards weaker growth and higher unemployment, Reid added. 

President Trump, however, said the Fed should cut interest rates as tariffs start to "ease" into the economy, after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said tariffs may delay further progress in bringing inflation to the 2% target. 

"The Fed is slowing the reduction of its balance sheet. This reflects the fact that liquidity demand is not slowing so aggressively, and should necessarily be seen as an easing," Donovan said.

Initial jobless claims rose slightly in the past week, in line with expectations, while U.S. current account deficit narrowed slightly in Q4, beating consensus.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: First Financial declares $0.51 dividend
data.description:
• First Financial (NASDAQ:THFF) declares $0.51/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.03%
  • Payable April 15; for shareholders of record April 1; ex-div April 1.
  • See THFF Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Academy Sports launches Jordan brand, offsets soft FY25 guidance, mixed Q4 results
data.description:
Academy Sports and Outdoors (NASDAQ:ASO) shares are gaining ground in early Thursday trading as the company’s announcement to launch the Jordan brand helped offset underwhelming guidance for the current quarter and mixed results for the current reported quarter.

“This year, we are introducing an exciting slate of new brands, including the launch of the Jordan brand with a focus on sport, with shops in men's, women's and kids. We're also bolstering our targeted marketing capabilities, and rolling out new technology across our stores, all designed to elevate our ability to serve our core customers. These innovations, combined with our unwavering commitment to value leadership, position us for a pivotal year in which we anticipate a return to topline sales growth,” Academy Sports and Outdoor CEO Steve Lawrence said.

Although new brands will ultimately increase foot traffic, the first quarter is expected to be the most challenging from a sales and earnings perspective as a result of the plan to open five stores and transition to the new Jordan floor set. These initiatives will likely start to positively impact results beginning in the second quarter.

Accordingly, Academy (NASDAQ:ASO) sets FY25 net sales to a range of $6.09B to $6.265B, a 4.2% improvement from FY24 but only slightly above the $6.24B estimate on the top end.

Comparable store sales are expected to be down 2.0% to up 1.0%, a 460 basis point gain from last year, while gross margin is expected to improve by 40 basis points to between 34.0% to 34.5%.

Adjusted EPS is targeted for $5.75 to $6.20 per share versus the $6.46 estimate, and adjusted cash flow will likely decline by 11% year-over-year to a range of $290M to $320M.

Shares are up 2% in premarket.

data.title: Nel Hydrogen receives order for MC500 containerized PEM electrolyser
data.description:
• Nel Hydrogen US, a subsidiary of Nel ASA (OTCPK:NLLSY) has received a purchase order for one 2.5 MW containerized PEM unit for the Aberdeen Hydrogen Hub project in the north-east of Scotland, the company said on Thursday.
  • The scalable green hydrogen production, storage and distribution facility, powered by renewable energy, is being delivered through a joint venture between bp and Aberdeen City Council.
  • Hydrasun awarded the contract following their successful bid to design and integrate the hydrogen refuelling station and associated infrastructure.
  • Hydrasun is an international provider of integrated fluid transfer, power, and control products, serving diverse industries worldwide.

data.title: Nvidia increasing use of HBM is positive for Micron, Citi says
data.description:
• Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) increased use of high-bandwidth memory in its upcoming line of GPUs is likely a positive for Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), investment firm Citi said.
  • "The new Rubin chip will contain 288GB of HBM4 DRAM, 50% more content compared to the current Blackwell generation," analyst Christopher Danely wrote in a note to clients. "We believe this is good news for Micron as we believe Micron’s [high-bandwidth memory] gross margins is roughly 70%."
  • Additionally, research firm TrendForce recently raised its price forecast for dynamic random access memory in the second-quarter. It now sees pricing up 3% to 8% sequentially, from a prior view of 0%, due to improved inventory dynamics. "We believe this supports our view that pricing should improve beginning in 2Q25," Danely, who has a Buy rating on Micron, added.
  • Micron is set to report quarterly results after the close of trading. A consensus of analysts expect the company to earn $1.42 per share on $7.9B in revenue.

data.title: SA Charts: Here are the biggest corporate spenders on buybacks in Q4
data.description:
S&P 500 companies posted a sequential uptick in buybacks during the fourth quarter to $243.2B from $226.6B in the previous one, according to S&P data. 

Meanwhile, 2024 expenditure on stock repurchases grew to a record 942.5B, up 18.5% from 2023's $795.2B.

The biggest S&P 500 firms continued the largest spending on buybacks. Allocating $26.6B, Apple (AAPL) continued to dominate stock repurchase issuances for the quarter. For 2024, Apple spent $104.2 billion on buybacks, up from 2023's $83.9 billion. 

Google-parent Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) spent about $15.6B on buybacks in Q4, similar to the $15.3B in the previous one. Meanwhile, NVIDIA (NVDA) saw a 23.6% drop in buyback spending to $9.7B. 

ExxonMobil (XOM) allocated $5.8B on stock repurchases, while Kroger spent $5.0B, a humongous jump from $9M in the third quarter. 

The top 20 S&P 500 companies accounted for 49.0% of Q4 buybacks, down from Q3's 53.2%, above the historical average of 47.7% and the pre-COVID historical average of 44.5%, S&P data showed.

Of the 500 companies on the index, 342 reported buybacks of at least $5 million, up from 332 in Q3 and 313 in Q4 last year.

Among sectors, technology firms continued to outpace stock repurchases compared to other industries. Second in the standing, financial stocks decreased buybacks by 4.4% to $42.1B in Q4. Meanwhile, Consumer Staples saw their buyback spend skyrocket by 97.9% from Q3 to $15.3B.

data.title: TOMI Environmental Solutions' technology gets selected by NASA
data.description:
• TOMI Environmental Solutions (NASDAQ:TOMZ) said it has deployed its SteraMist iHP technology at the NASA Johnson Space Center, as the company expands into the aerospace sector.
  • SteraMist’s iHP technology is a disinfectant that uses cold plasma and a low percentage hydrogen peroxide blend solution.
  • The technology has been selected by NASA in partnership with a U.S. government and commercial services engineering contractor.

data.title: LiveOne exceeds 1.3M subscribers and ad-supported users
data.description:
• LiveOne (NASDAQ:LVO) a music, entertainment, and technology platform said on Thursday that it has surpassed 1.3M subscribers and ad-supported users, a 130% increase since January 1, 2025.
  • The company also unveiled multi-tier revenue drivers leveraging a 12-person sales team, DAX partnership, and AI marketing and Tesla partnership to convert to paid subscribers.

data.title: RWE AG GAAP EPS of €3.12, revenue of €24.22B
data.description:
• RWE AG press release (OTCPK:RWEOY): FY GAAP EPS of €3.12.
